tool_name: Arc Welding Heat Input, HAZ Width & Preheat Temperature Calculation

inputs.name: Process Thermal Efficiency Factor (η)
inputs.note: AWS D1.1-2020 Commentary §C-4.11 / EN 1011-1 Table B.1

inputs.name: Diffusible Hydrogen Content of Weld Metal (H_d)

formulas: HI_kj_mm = (arc_voltage_v * welding_current_a * thermal_efficiency * 60) / (travel_speed_mm_min * 1000)   // [kJ/mm] Arc Energy (Heat Input per Unit Length) Reference: AWS D1.1-2020 §4.11 / EN 1011-1:2009 §7
HAZ_mm = 0.9 * HI_kj_mm / (4.13e-3 * plate_thickness_mm)   // [mm] Estimated HAZ Width (Rosenthal 2D Model) Reference: Rosenthal (1946) thin-plate 2D heat flow; applicable when t < HAZ_mm/2

CET = base_metal_CE * 0.75 + 0.25; T_preheat_c = 350 * Math.sqrt(CET - 0.1 * Math.log10(HI_kj_mm * 1000) + 0.015 * Math.log10(hydrogen_content) + 0.005 * Math.log10(plate_thickness_mm)) - 0.08 * plate_thickness_mm   // [°C] Preheat Temperature — CET Method (EN 1011-2 Annex C) Reference: EN 1011-2:2001 Annex C — CET method (simplified; contact WPS engineer for full calculation)

T_preheat_aws_c = (base_metal_CE < 0.40) ? 10 : (base_metal_CE < 0.45) ? 66 : (base_metal_CE < 0.50) ? 107 : 150   // [°C] AWS D1.1 Preheat (CE-based, Table 3.2) Reference: AWS D1.1-2020 Table 3.2 — minimum preheat based on CE and thickness category
T_preheat_gov_c = Math.max(T_preheat_c, T_preheat_aws_c, 10)   // [°C] Governing Preheat Temperature Reference: Conservative: maximum of EN 1011-2 and AWS D1.1 requirements; minimum 10°C

t85_s = (6700 - 5 * T_preheat_gov_c) * HI_kj_mm * ((1/(300 - T_preheat_gov_c))^2 - (1/(700 - T_preheat_gov_c))^2) * 1e-3   // [s] Cooling Rate Indicator (t8/5 Proxy) Reference: Seyffarth & Kassatkin cooling time t8/5 — thin-plate approximation; governs HAZ hardness

V1.error_msg: Heat input > 3.5 kJ/mm: excessive for most structural steels — grain coarsening and toughness loss in HAZ

V2.error_msg: Heat input < 0.3 kJ/mm: very high cooling rate — hard HAZ and hydrogen cracking risk even with preheat

V3.error_msg: High CE + high hydrogen: severe cold cracking risk — use H5 or H2.5 consumables and increase preheat by 30°C

smart_warnings.message: Preheat > 150°C: verify interpass temperature control — max interpass typically 250°C for structural steels
CE > 0.50: high cold cracking susceptibility — post-weld hydrogen release bake (200°C × 2h) strongly recommended per EN 1011-2
HAZ width > 8 mm: large heat-affected zone — CTOD toughness testing at HAZ required per BS 7448-2
t8/5 < 5 s: rapid cooling — risk of martensite formation in HAZ; verify hardness < 350 HV per ISO 15614-1
GTAW at high current: consider GMAW for productivity — TIG heat input advantage diminishes above 200 A
